title: Market Close
commentary: The Iran oil shock delivers its verdict—Dow closes down 1,077 points (-2.2%) at 47,671, S&P -1.2% at 6,785, worst day in three weeks as WTI crude breaches $81.62 (+9.3%). Safe havens implode counterintuitively: gold -1.4% at $5,067, silver crushed -3.0% as surging dollar (+0.6% at 99.32) siphons all defensive flows. Crypto correlates perfectly with risk: Bitcoin -2.3% at $70,975, Ethereum -4.8% at $2,075—the 'digital gold' narrative dead on arrival. The tape's message is brutal: when oil spikes and the dollar rips, nothing works.

title: Late Afternoon
commentary: $80 oil is here—WTI breaches the psychological level to $81.09 (+8.6%), highest since October, as Iran escalation enters a new phase. Dow collapses 1,053 points (-2.2%) to 47,695, S&P -1.3% at 6,780, Nasdaq -1.2%—yesterday's gains obliterated and then some. Russell 2000 carnage at -2.5%; small caps now deep in correction territory. Crypto no safe haven: Bitcoin -3.0% at $71,000, Ethereum -2.4% at $2,075 tracking equities tick for tick. Dollar holds +0.46% at 99.22—the only winner in a sea of red. Two hours to the bell and no sign of dip-buying.

title: Afternoon
commentary: The rout deepens—Dow now down 972 points (-2.0%) at 47,776, S&P -1.3% at 6,780, Nasdaq -1.2% as the Iran oil shock reverberates through everything. Russell 2000 in free fall at -2.6%—small caps utterly destroyed. Oil holds near highs at $79.57 (+6.6%), just pennies from $80; dollar +0.45% at 99.22 continues to crush risk assets. Crypto correlates with stocks for once: Bitcoin -3.3% at $70,809, Ethereum -3.1% at $2,059—the 'digital gold' narrative nowhere to be found. Gold can't rally either near $5,070 (-1.3%); nothing works when the dollar is a wrecking ball.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Full capitulation mode—Dow craters 772 points (-1.6%) to 47,976, erasing the entire week's gains as oil touches $80 for the first time since October (+6.2% at $79.32, intraday high $79.97). S&P -0.65% at 6,825, Russell 2000 crushed -1.9% with small caps in freefall. Dollar surges +0.5% to 99.23—a wrecking ball for everything else; gold can't find a bid near $5,085 (-1.0%) despite peak fear. Crypto bleeding out: Bitcoin -2.5% to $71,341, Ethereum -2.1% at $2,080. This is what stagflation fears look like—oil up, everything else down.

title: Midday
commentary: Carnage accelerates—Dow craters 636 points (-1.3%) to 48,112 as oil hits a fresh 52-week high at $78.67 (+5.4%); yesterday's optimism evaporates in an hour. S&P -0.5% at 6,833, Nasdaq -0.2% with tech showing relative resilience; small caps obliterated with Russell -1.5%. Crypto joins the risk-off rout: Bitcoin -2.3% to $71,481, Ethereum -2.0% at $2,081 as correlations tighten. Gold can't catch a bid despite chaos—down -0.9% near $5,090 as surging dollar (+0.45% to 99.21) siphons safe-haven flows. The Iran premium is back with a vengeance.

title: Morning Session
commentary: Oil rips to session highs +4.6% at $78.08 as Iran airstrikes intensify—Dow sinks 275 points to 48,473, S&P flat at 6,865, Nasdaq +0.2% with tech holding in better. The surprise: gold drops -0.65% to $5,102 as dollar strength (+0.3% to 99.05) trumps safe-haven flows; unusual divergence from oil. Crypto holds steady—Bitcoin $72,527 flat, Ethereum -0.6% at $2,112 as digital assets decouple from war headlines. Small caps getting crushed with Russell 2000 -1%. The tape is telling us: energy inflation fears > flight to safety.

title: Market Open
commentary: Yesterday's rally meets reality at the open—Dow flat at 48,739, S&P unchanged at 6,869, Nasdaq flat at 22,807 as Iran peace hopes evaporate overnight. Oil spikes +3.0% to $76.93 with fresh airstrikes reigniting Hormuz supply fears; the geopolitical risk premium is back. Crypto retreats from yesterday's euphoria: Bitcoin -1.0% to $72,484, Ethereum -1.9% at $2,117 as risk-off returns. Gold catches a bid +0.5% near $5,160, dollar strengthens +0.3% to 99.1. Asia bounced overnight (Nikkei +1.9%) but US futures couldn't hold—traders bracing for another volatile session.

title: After Hours
commentary: Wall Street closes out a powerful recovery session—Dow +0.5% at 48,748, S&P 500 +0.8% at 6,870, Nasdaq +1.3% at 22,809 as Iran war fatigue sets in and bulls take control. Crypto continues its tear: Bitcoin +7.3% near $73,200, Ethereum +8.9% at $2,158—digital assets emerging as the unlikely winners of geopolitical chaos. Oil edges higher +0.9% to $75.26 with Hormuz fears contained; gold +1.0% at $5,135 holds gains but momentum shifts to risk assets. Dollar sinks -0.3% to 98.77 as markets digest Trump's Warsh Fed pick. Asia opens next—Nikkei futures pointing lower after today's -3.6% rout.

title: Market Close
commentary: Bulls clinch a decisive victory on Day 5 of the Iran conflict—S&P 500 closes +1.0% at 6,884, Nasdaq +1.6% to 22,865, Dow +0.6% at 48,812 as geopolitical fears fade into the rearview. Crypto steals the show: Ethereum explodes +10% to $2,181, Bitcoin +8% near $73,650 with the digital asset complex completely shrugging off Middle East tensions. Gold settles +1.0% at $5,137, oil flat at $74.57—the safe-haven premium has evaporated. Dollar continues retreat -0.3% to 98.76 as Trump's Warsh Fed nomination adds a new variable. The market's message is clear: buy the dip, ignore the headlines.

title: Late Afternoon
commentary: Equities grind to fresh session highs into the final hours—S&P +1.0% at 6,883, Nasdaq +1.6% at 22,885 (best levels since late February), Dow +0.7%. Oil extends recovery +0.8% to $75.12 as traders recalibrate Middle East risk; the morning lows at $73.28 now distant memory. Crypto maintains gains with Ethereum +9.3% at $2,165 session high, Bitcoin +5.2% near $72,000 on persistent spot demand. Precious metals consolidate: gold +0.4% at $5,143, silver +0.1% at $83.59—taking a backseat to risk assets. Dollar -0.15% at 98.91; two hours to the bell with bulls firmly in control.
